title: Modon Kira
content:
{{Top|ffa|leg}}
{{Youmay|the leader of the [[Beast Riders]] during the [[Beast Wars]]|the [[Modon Kira (Galactic Civil War)|King]] of [[Onderon/Legends|Onderon]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]}}

{{Quote|I am ready to talk peace…but I'm also ready to make war!|Modon Kira|<ref name="beastwars2" />}}
'''Modon Kira''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] native of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Onderon/Legends|Onderon]] and a respected and powerful [[Beast-Lord]] on his [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] during the final years of the [[Beast Wars|war]] between the Onderonian [[Beast Riders]] and the [[Naddist army|military]] of the walled [[City/Legends|city]] of [[Iziz/Legends|Iziz]]. Modon was the son of [[Drokko Kira]] and in turn had a son, [[Oron Kira]], who fell in love with [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Galia]], heiress to the throne of Iziz. The two arranged to be [[Binding Ceremony|married]] at [[Kira Fortress|Fortress Kira]], Modon Kira's forest stronghold, and the Beast-[[Lord/Legends|Lord]] attended [[Binding Ceremony of Oron Kira and Galia|their binding ceremony]]. The union was subsequently interrupted by the entrance of three [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]]—brothers [[Ulic Qel-Droma/Legends|Ulic]] and [[Cay Qel-Droma]], and the [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] [[Tott Doneeta]]—who had been sent by Galia's mother, [[Queen/Legends|Queen]] [[Amanoa]], to retrieve the princess. Instead, Kira convinced the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] to join his side, and they embarked on a peace mission to convince Amanoa to end the war.

The peace mission failed, however, and Modon Kira was forced to lead his [[Beast Rider army]] against Iziz in a [[Battle of Iziz|climactic battle]]. Although the Beast Riders took the upper hand, Amanoa turned the tide of the battle by invoking the power of the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] against Kira's [[Army/Legends|army]]. The momentum shifted, and Kira's forces were put on the defensive. The Beast Riders regained the advantage, however, when [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Arca Jeth]] arrived and employed his skill with [[Battle meditation/Legends|battle meditation]], which proved to be the deciding factor. After the battle, Modon's son, Oron, and Galia were married under the laws of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], officially unifying the two warring civilizations of Onderon.

==Biography==
===Early life and son's marriage===
{{Quote|Hold your fire, men! These must be the Jedi Galia told us about—will you lay down your weapons and join us for a wedding banquet, Jedi? I am told you are here to bring peace.|Modon Kira|<ref name="beastwars2" />}}
Modon Kira, the son of [[Beast Riders|Beast Rider]] [[Drokko Kira]], was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] born on the [[Inner Rim/Legends|Inner Rim]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Onderon/Legends|Onderon]]. Modon's father had been [[Exile (punishment)/Legends|cast]] out of Onderon's [[Capital/Legends|capital]], the walled [[City/Legends|city]] of [[Iziz/Legends|Iziz]], [[4050 BBY|years before]] for challenging the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] [[Naddist|regime]] of [[Queen/Legends|Queen]] [[Amanoa]] and the worshipers of an ancient [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] named [[Freedon Nadd]]. Drokko promptly joined the exiled Beast Riders of Onderon's wilderness who were at war with Iziz's inhabitants. Modon grew to become a proud and ruthless man of formidable physical strength, and in time became a respected and powerful [[Beast-Lord]]. He also became a successful [[Commander/Legends|commander]] in the ongoing [[Beast Wars]] against the forces of Iziz.<ref name="beastwars1">[[Tales of the Jedi 1|''Tales of the Jedi'' 1]]</ref>

Modon eventually had a son, [[Oron Kira]], who also became a respected Beast-[[Lord/Legends|Lord]]. As enemies of the [[Iziz ruling family|Onderon Royal Family]], Beast Riders were not allowed to enter the city. Nevertheless, Modon and his family traveled in and out of the city for years, trading secretly with the [[Politics/Legends|political]] underground. It was there that Oron fell in love with [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Galia]], heiress to the Onderonian throne, who returned his affections. The two made plans to [[Marriage/Legends|marry]], although it meant that she would be forced to forsake her crown and royal lineage.<ref name="beastwars2" /> In [[4000 BBY/Legends|4000 BBY]], Modon's son and a team of Beast Riders staged an [[Raid on the Iziz Royal Palace|attack]] on the [[Iziz Royal Palace]], where they absconded with Galia.<ref name="beastwars1" />

[[File:BindingCeremony.jpg|thumb|left|275px|Modon Kira with his family and Galia at the wedding banquet]]
Galia was brought to [[Kira Fortress|Fortress Kira]], Modon forest citadel, where the [[Binding Ceremony|binding ceremony]] of the Beast-Lord's son and the Princess of Iziz commenced. Modon, his elderly father, Drokko, and hundreds of Beast Riders attended the festivities. However, [[Binding Ceremony of Oron Kira and Galia|the ceremony]] was interrupted by the arrival of three [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]]—brothers [[Ulic Qel-Droma/Legends|Ulic]] and [[Cay Qel-Droma]], and the [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] [[Tott Doneeta]]—who had been sent by Galia's mother, Amanoa, to retrieve the princess. The [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] swept into the fortress riding [[boma]] beasts and grabbed Galia, although they quickly unhanded her when she revealed that she was willingly marrying Oron.<ref name="beastwars2" />

Modon, having previously been informed of the Jedi's approach, welcomed them and invited them to join the festivities. Oron informed the Jedi that the Onderonian monarchy was under the sway of the dark side, and Modon revealed that he had gathered the Beast Rider forces to launch one final assault on Iziz. The Beast-Lord asked the Jedi to join the attack, although Ulic Qel-Droma still wanted to attempt to peacefully resolve the conflict. The Beast Riders and the Jedi reached an agreement: the Jedi, along with Galia and Oron, would go to the Iziz Royal Palace and try to convince Amanoa to end the war, and Modon's [[Beast Rider army|army of Beast Riders]] would attack Iziz if they failed.<ref name="beastwars2" />

===The Battle of Iziz===
{{Quote|The Jedi has failed! Let the attack begin!|Modon Kira|<ref name="beastwars2" />}}
[[File:BeastRiders.jpg|thumb|right|270px|Modon Kira rallies his warriors for the coming battle.]]
Modon's Beast Rider [[Army/Legends|army]] trailed behind on their winged [[Drexl|warbeasts]] while the Jedi, Oron, and Galia flew to the Iziz Royal Palace in the ''[[Nebulon Ranger]]'' [[Starship/Legends|starship]]. Modon believed in his heart that the peace mission would fail, and he appeared to be correct as the [[Iziz defense tower|defense guns]] on the city walls targeted the Beast Riders on sight. In the royal palace, Amanoa rejected the plea for peace and gave herself over to the dark side, initiating battle in the throne room. Waiting outside of Iziz, Modon received a communication from Galia that the peace mission had failed.<ref name="beastwars2" />

With war the only option, Kira rallied his Beast Rider army and [[Battle of Iziz|laid siege]] to Iziz. Kira's Beast Riders pushed [[Naddist army|Amanoa's army]] back to the walls of the Royal Palace, forcing Amanoa to withdraw to the [[Tomb of Freedon Nadd|tomb]] of Amanoa's [[Sith Master/Legends|Sith Master]], the deceased Dark Lord Freedon Nadd. There, Amanoa [[Waves of darkness|called the dark side down]] upon the Beast Riders. The Beast Riders' progress was halted by the pall of the dark side, and despite the tide being turned, Kira ordered his army to fight on. Their [[Drexl|warbeasts]] had grown out of control, and the ground [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]] felt sick and confused.<ref name="beastwars2" />

Although the Beast Rider force had been pushed into a steady retreat and destruction appeared imminent, defeat was averted when [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Arca Jeth]], who had originally sent the three Jedi to resolve the conflict on Onderon, appeared in the Onderon skies in [[SunGem|his starship]]. Jeth utilized the exotic [[Force power/Legends|Force power]] known as [[Battle meditation/Legends|battle meditation]], which boosted the confidence of Kira's army while demoralizing Amanoa's defenders. The usage of Jeth's battle meditation proved to be the tipping point of the battle, and Iziz fell to Modon Kira's Beast Rider army. After the Battle of Iziz, the marriage of Oron Kira and Princess Galia was consecrated under the laws of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], officially unifying the two warring tribes of Onderon.<ref name="beastwars2" />

===Legacy===
[[11 ABY/Legends|Thousands of years]] after the final battle of the Beast Wars,<ref name="empire's end">[[Empire's End 2|''Empire's End'' 2]]</ref> one of Modon Kira's descendants,<ref name="cswe">''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''</ref> also named [[Modon Kira (Galactic Civil War)|Modon Kira]], was the [[King/Legends|King]] of Onderon. During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], this man assisted Jedi Knights [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] and [[Empatojayos Brand]] and their friend [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] [[Battle of Onderon (Galactic Civil War)|attack]] [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] forces on the planet.<ref name="empire's end" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|I will ''not'' give in—we've committed everything! Keep fighting!|Modon Kira|<ref name="beastwars2" />}}
A powerful and respected Beast-Lord on Onderon, Modon Kira was a proud and ruthless man of great physical strength. Kira passionately hated the established government of Iziz, believing that the monarchy's worshiping of the dark side of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] had corrupted Onderon. Additionally, his loathing for the monarchy was partly due to his father's banishment from Iziz for challenging the dark side. Nevertheless, Kira supported his son's love for Princess Galia, hoping a marriage between the two could end the Beast Wars and finally bring peace to Onderon.

Kira was, however, first and foremost a warrior. All his life he had made war, and even though he assented to Ulic Qel-Droma's plan to try and end the war peacefully, Kira firmly believed that the Jedi would fail and eagerly looked forward to the battle to come. Kira took great pride in the Beast Rider force he had assembled for the Battle of Iziz, and when he was informed of the peace mission's failure he excitedly broke the news to his forces. After Queen Amanoa summoned the dark side down against the Beast Riders during the battle, despite the drastic situation they were in, Kira refused to give in, as he had committed everything to the attack.<ref name="beastwars2" />

==Behind the scenes==
Modon Kira first appears in ''[[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi – Ulic Qel-Droma and the Beast Wars of Onderon]]'', the first story arc in the [[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi (comic series)|''Tales of the Jedi'']] [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] series, written by [[Tom Veitch]] and released in [[1993]]. Kira only appears in the [[Tales of the Jedi 2|second and final issue]] of the arc, wherein he plays a major role. In this appearance Kira is illustrated by [[Chris Gossett]].<ref name="beastwars2" /> Kira is later mentioned in [[Tales of the Jedi – The Freedon Nadd Uprising 1|both]] [[Tales of the Jedi – The Freedon Nadd Uprising 2|issues]] of ''[[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi – The Freedon Nadd Uprising]]'', a story arc of ''Tales of the Jedi'' released in [[1994]].<ref name="uprising">''[[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi – The Freedon Nadd Uprising]]''</ref> Although Kira plays a relatively large part in ''Ulic Qel-Droma and the Beast Wars of Onderon'', he is entirely absent from the [[Tales of the Jedi (audio drama)|''Tales of the Jedi'' audio adaptation]], which recounts the events of that story arc. Instead, all of Kira's lines are attributed to his son, Oron.<ref name="audio">[[Tales of the Jedi (audio drama)|''Tales of the Jedi'' audio drama]]</ref>
